naturalMeaning: The safe all-purpose 'hello'; 'xin' softens it politely and it works with any listener.

examples.text: Xin chào! Rất vui được gặp bạn.

naturalMeaning: Used both for hi and bye; normally paired with an address word, e.g. 'chào anh'.
A slightly formal 'goodbye'; both syllables take the heavy nặng tone.
The standard thanks; 'cảm' is hỏi tone. Add an address word to sound warmer: 'cảm ơn anh'.
Both apologises and gets attention; 'lỗi' carries the ngã (broken-rising) tone.

examples.text: Xin lỗi, nhà vệ sinh ở đâu?
examples.translation: Excuse me, where is the toilet?

naturalMeaning: The usual reply to thanks; literally 'there isn't anything'.
Polite Northern 'yes' to agree or acknowledge someone senior (Southern uses 'dạ').
A respectful softener said before answering an elder; nặng tone, short and low.
Adds 'please' to a request; 'làm' is huyền (low-falling) tone.

examples.text: Làm ơn cho tôi xem thực đơn.
examples.translation: Please let me see the menu.

naturalMeaning: Neutral 'I' with strangers or in writing; sounds a bit distant with family or friends.
'You' for someone your own age, and also the word for 'friend'; nặng tone.
Address a slightly older or peer man as 'anh'; the same word means 'older brother'.

examples.translation: Are you well? (to an older man)

naturalMeaning: Address a slightly older woman as 'chị' (nặng tone); also means 'older sister'.

examples.translation: Where are you going? (to an older woman)

naturalMeaning: Used for anyone younger than you, and how the younger person calls themselves back.
Address an old man or a grandfather as 'ông'; huyền tone.
Address an old woman or a grandmother as 'bà'; huyền tone (contrast 'ba' = three).

examples.text: Bà ơi, bà có khỏe không?

naturalMeaning: Means 'child'; a child also calls themselves 'con' when speaking to parents.

translation: grandchild / niece / nephew / I (to an elder)
naturalMeaning: How a young person refers to themselves with a much older person; sắc tone.
Exclusive 'we' that leaves out the listener; 'chúng ta' includes them instead.
General 'they' for other people; nặng tone.
'Zero'; the very same word 'không' also means 'no / not'.
'One'; nặng tone. Becomes 'mốt' in some compounds like twenty-one.
'Two'; level ngang tone.
'Three'; level tone (contrast 'bà' = grandmother, huyền).
'Four'; sắc (high-rising) tone.
'Five'; the same word 'năm' also means 'year'.
'Six'; sắc tone.
'Seven'; hỏi (dipping) tone.
'Eight'; sắc tone.
'Nine'; sắc tone. Also means 'ripe / cooked'.
'Ten'; huyền tone. Note 'mười' (10) vs 'mươi' (the -ty tens marker).
Ten + one; teens are 'mười' plus the digit.
Ten + two.
Fifteen uses 'lăm', not 'năm', for the 5 after a ten.
Twenty is 'hai mươi' with 'mươi' in the level tone (the tens marker).
'Hundred'; usually said as 'một trăm' for one hundred.
Northern 'thousand' (Southern says 'ngàn'); huyền tone.
The whole family unit; 'đình' takes huyền tone.

examples.text: Gia đình tôi có bốn người.

naturalMeaning: Northern word for 'dad' (Southern says 'ba'); sắc tone.
'Mom'; nặng tone, short and low.
'con trai' = son or a boy; 'con gái' = daughter or a girl.
'Daughter' or 'girl'; 'gái' is sắc tone.
'Wife'; nặng tone.
'Husband'; huyền tone.
'Person / people'; also names nationality, e.g. 'người Việt' = a Vietnamese person.

examples.translation: There are many people at the market.

naturalMeaning: A calendar day; huyền tone.

examples.text: Một ngày có hai mươi bốn giờ.

naturalMeaning: 'Today'; a core time word for present-day plans.
The morning period; 'buổi' (hỏi) marks a part of the day.

examples.text: Buổi sáng tôi uống cà phê.
examples.translation: In the morning I drink coffee.

naturalMeaning: The afternoon; 'chiều' is huyền tone.

examples.translation: In the afternoon I go to the market.

naturalMeaning: The evening; 'tối' also means 'dark'.

examples.text: Buổi tối tôi ăn cơm ở nhà.
examples.translation: In the evening I eat at home.

naturalMeaning: Both 'hour' and 'o'clock'; huyền tone (contrast 'gió' = wind).
Literally 'the second'; the Vietnamese week counts days from Monday.

examples.translation: On Monday I go to work.

naturalMeaning: 'Third day' = Tuesday.
Wednesday uses 'tư', not 'bốn', for the fourth day.

examples.translation: On Wednesday I meet a friend.

naturalMeaning: 'Fifth day' = Thursday.

examples.text: Thứ năm chị có bận không?

naturalMeaning: 'Sixth day' = Friday.

examples.text: Thứ sáu tôi đi ăn phở.
examples.translation: On Friday I go eat pho.

naturalMeaning: 'Seventh day' = Saturday.
Sunday breaks the pattern: 'chủ nhật', not 'thứ tám'.
Cooked rice, and by extension a meal; 'ăn cơm' = to eat / have a meal.
Vietnam's famous rice-noodle soup; hỏi tone (contrast 'phố' = street).
Both plain bread and the filled baguette sandwich.

examples.text: Cho tôi một cái bánh mì.

naturalMeaning: General word for meat; nặng tone.
The bird and its meat; huyền tone.
Fish (the food or the animal); sắc tone (contrast 'cà' = eggplant).
'Egg'; sắc tone.
General word for vegetables / greens; level tone.

examples.text: Ăn nhiều rau tốt cho sức khỏe.
examples.translation: Eating lots of vegetables is healthy.

naturalMeaning: 'Water'; the same word 'nước' also means 'country'.

examples.translation: Give me a glass of water.

naturalMeaning: Vietnamese coffee, usually strong; 'cà' is huyền tone.

examples.text: Tôi uống cà phê buổi sáng.
examples.translation: I drink coffee in the morning.

naturalMeaning: 'Tea'; huyền tone.
A sit-down restaurant; both syllables huyền tone.
The menu; 'thực' is nặng tone.
'Table'; huyền tone (contrast 'bạn' = you/friend, 'bán' = to sell).
A pair of chopsticks; ngã tone.

examples.text: Cho tôi thêm một đôi đũa.
examples.translation: Give me one more pair of chopsticks.

naturalMeaning: Northern word for a bowl (Southern says 'chén'); sắc tone.
'Money'; huyền tone.
The dong; prices are counted in thousands of đồng.

examples.text: Cái này năm mươi nghìn đồng.

naturalMeaning: A shop or store; 'cửa' is hỏi tone.

examples.text: Cửa hàng mở cửa lúc tám giờ.

naturalMeaning: A supermarket; 'thị' is nặng tone.

examples.text: Tôi đi siêu thị mua rau.
examples.translation: I go to the supermarket to buy vegetables.

naturalMeaning: 'Price'; sắc tone. 'giá bao nhiêu?' = how much is the price?
Both a house and 'home'; 'ở nhà' = to be / stay home.
A traditional market; nặng tone (contrast 'cho' = to give).

examples.translation: Mom goes to the market to buy fish.

naturalMeaning: A hospital; both syllables nặng tone.

examples.text: Bệnh viện ở gần đây không?

naturalMeaning: A railway station; 'ga' is borrowed from French 'gare'.
A bus or coach station; 'bến' (sắc) means a terminal or wharf.

examples.text: Tôi đi taxi đến bến xe.
examples.translation: I take a taxi to the bus station.

naturalMeaning: The polite word for toilet; literally 'hygiene house'.

examples.text: Nhà vệ sinh ở đâu ạ?

naturalMeaning: A school; 'trường' huyền, 'học' nặng (also the verb 'to study').

examples.translation: The school is near my house.

naturalMeaning: An airport; literally 'flying yard'.

examples.translation: The airport is far from the center.

naturalMeaning: 'Road / street'; the same word 'đường' also means 'sugar'.
'On the left'; 'trái' is sắc tone.
'On the right'; 'phải' is hỏi tone (the same word can mean 'must').

examples.translation: My house is on the right.

naturalMeaning: 'Straight ahead'; hỏi tone. 'đi thẳng' = go straight.
'Here / this place'; level tone.

examples.translation: This is my house / Here is my house.

naturalMeaning: 'There / that'; sắc tone.
'On / above / up'; level tone.

examples.translation: The book is on the table.

naturalMeaning: 'Under / below / down'; sắc tone.

examples.translation: The cat is under the chair.

naturalMeaning: 'Near'; huyền tone. Opposite is 'xa' (far).

examples.translation: The market is near my house.

naturalMeaning: The motorbike, Vietnam's main vehicle; 'máy' is sắc tone.

examples.text: Tôi đi xe máy đi làm.
examples.translation: I ride a motorbike to work.

naturalMeaning: A bicycle; 'đạp' (nặng) means 'to pedal'.

examples.text: Em đi xe đạp đến trường.
examples.translation: I ride a bicycle to school.

naturalMeaning: Northern word for 'car' (Southern often says 'xe hơi').

examples.text: Nhà tôi có một cái ô tô.

naturalMeaning: A city bus; 'buýt' is from French 'bus'.

examples.text: Tôi đi xe buýt số năm.

naturalMeaning: 'Tàu' covers trains and larger boats; huyền tone.
An airplane; literally 'flying machine'.

examples.text: Tôi đi máy bay về nhà.

naturalMeaning: 'Head'; huyền tone (contrast 'đau' = to hurt, level tone).
'Eye'; sắc tone (contrast 'mặt' = face, nặng).
'Mouth'; nặng tone.
'Tay' covers both hand and arm; level tone.
'Chân' covers both leg and foot; level tone.
'To hurt / ache'; level tone (contrast 'đầu' = head, huyền).
A medical doctor; 'bác' sắc, 'sĩ' ngã.

examples.translation: I need to see a doctor.

naturalMeaning: 'Medicine'; sắc tone. 'uống thuốc' = to take medicine.
'Weather'; 'thời' huyền, 'tiết' sắc.
'Rain'; level tone (contrast 'mua' = to buy).
'Sunshine / sunny'; sắc tone. 'trời nắng' = it's sunny.
'Wind'; sắc tone (contrast 'giờ' = hour, huyền).
'Sky', and the subject word for weather: 'trời mưa' = it rains.

examples.translation: The sky is so beautiful today.

naturalMeaning: 'To go'; also used before another verb to mean setting off to do it.
'To come / arrive / reach'; sắc tone. Also 'to' a place.
'To eat'; 'ăn cơm' literally 'eat rice' means to have a meal.

examples.translation: What do you want to eat?

naturalMeaning: 'To drink'; sắc tone.
'To want'; sắc tone. Put it before another verb: 'muốn đi' = want to go.
'To have / there is'; also the yes half of 'có... không?' questions.
The 'to be' that links two nouns: 'A là B'; huyền tone. Not used before adjectives.
'To like'; sắc tone.
'To see' or 'to feel / find'; sắc tone.

examples.translation: I saw you at the market.

naturalMeaning: 'To give'; also 'let me' in requests: 'cho tôi...' = give me / let me have...

examples.translation: Give me a glass of water.

naturalMeaning: 'To buy'; level tone (contrast 'mưa' = rain).
'To sell'; sắc tone (contrast 'bàn' = table, 'bạn' = friend).

translation: to do / to make / to work
naturalMeaning: 'To do / make / work'; huyền tone. 'làm gì?' = do what?
'To speak / say'; sắc tone. 'nói tiếng Việt' = speak Vietnamese.

examples.text: Tôi nói tiếng Việt một chút.

naturalMeaning: 'To know (a fact or how to)'; sắc tone.
Both the verb 'to be located' and 'at / in'; hỏi tone.
Everyday word for 'big'; level tone. (More formal synonym: 'lớn'.)
'Small'; hỏi tone.
'Hot' (temperature); sắc tone.
'Cold'; nặng tone.
'Good'; sắc tone.
'Delicious'; level tone. The go-to word to praise food.

examples.translation: The pho here is very delicious.

naturalMeaning: Northern word for 'expensive' (Southern says 'mắc'); sắc tone.
'Cheap'; hỏi tone.

examples.translation: At the market, vegetables are very cheap.

naturalMeaning: 'Beautiful / pretty'; nặng tone.
'Ugly', or 'bad' for quality / character; sắc tone.
'New'; sắc tone. Also 'just (recently)' before a verb.
'Who'; level tone. Usually stays where the answer would go.
'What'; huyền tone. 'cái gì' = what thing.
'Where'; level tone. 'ở đâu?' = where at?
'When'; put 'bao giờ' first for the future, last for the past.
'Why'; 'tại' is nặng tone. Answered with 'vì' (because).

examples.text: Tại sao bạn học tiếng Việt?

naturalMeaning: 'How / in what way'; 'thế' sắc, 'nào' huyền.
'How much / how many' for larger amounts and prices.
'How many' for small counts, and also 'a few'; sắc tone. 'mấy giờ?' = what time?
